David Ellison, Skydance Media's chief and son of Larry Ellison, has taken over as Paramount Global's CEO via an $8 billion merger, blending tech innovation with Hollywood assets like Paramount Pictures and CBS. He pledges tech-driven content, journalistic integrity, and AI enhancements amid cable declines. Challenges include regulatory hurdles and industry disruptions, but his vision aims to revitalize the company. **David Ellison Takes Paramount CEO Role in $8B Skydance Merger**. In the high-stakes world of media conglomerates, David Ellison’s ascent to the helm of Paramount Global marks a pivotal shift, blending Silicon Valley innovation with Hollywood legacy. founder Larry Ellison, the 42-year-old Skydance Media chief has orchestrated an $8 billion merger that positions him as the new steward of iconic assets like Paramount Pictures and CBS. This deal, finalized amid regulatory scrutiny and industry upheaval, underscores Ellison’s ambition to revitalize a company grappling with declining cable revenues and streaming wars. Ellison’s initial outreach included a notable visit to CBS News offices, where he addressed staff amid anxieties over the merger’s implications for journalistic independence. Employees described the interaction as reassuring, with Ellison praising the network’s “long tradition of impactful reporting” and acknowledging the “challenging period” it has endured, including recent turmoil in its news division. Despite these hurdles, the merger closed on August 7, 2025, paving the way for Ellison to assume the CEO role. In a swift leadership shakeup, Ellison has assembled a new executive team, blending Skydance loyalists with select Paramount holdovers. Beyond news, Ellison’s blueprint for Paramount involves leveraging artificial intelligence to enhance movie production and exploring mergers and acquisitions to bolster its portfolio. Insights from The Los Angeles Times reveal Ellison’s hands-on approach in his first press meeting, where he addressed late-night programming and potential asset sales, aiming to position the company as a tech-savvy contender against giants like Netflix and Disney. Ellison’s team is eyeing cost synergies and content monetization, potentially through enhanced streaming services like Paramount+. David Ellison, CEO of Paramount Skydance, led the 2025 merger with Paramount Global and subsequent bid for Warner Bros. David Ellison founded Skydance Media, a studio behind some of the most commercially successful films of the early 21st century. The merger resulted in the formation of Paramount Skydance Corporation, with David Ellison as CEO and chair, and Jeff Shell as president. Ellison later oversaw the company’s expansion into television, animation, and interactive entertainment and led the merger between Skydance and Paramount Global, becoming chief executive officer of the newly formed **Paramount Skydance** (PSKY) in 2025.","score":0.8729662,"raw_content":null,"domain":"britannica.com","popularity_score":9.889519016642058,"main_topic":"people research"},{"url":"https://stocktwits.com/news-articles/markets/equity/paramount-names-david-ellison-ceo-following-merger-with-skydance/chrXZ7eRdTF","title":"Paramount Names David Ellison CEO Following Merger With Skydance","content":"Title: Paramount Names David Ellison CEO Following Merger With Skydance\nHomeNewsMarketsEquityParamount Names David Ellison CEO Following Merger With Skydance. # Paramount Names David Ellison CEO Following Merger With Skydance. Under Ellison’s leadership, Skydance released films like ‘Top Gun: Maverick,’ ‘Mission Impossible: Dead Reckoning’ (Parts 1 and 2), and ‘World War Z,’ among others. In this photo illustration, the logo of Paramount Global is displayed on a smartphone screen. Paramount Global (PARA) on Monday said David Ellison will be the CEO of the new entity once the company’s merger with Skydance Media is completed. Ellison is currently the CEO of Skydance Media, the company he co-founded in 2010. Under Ellison’s leadership, Skydance released films like ‘Top Gun: Maverick,’ ‘Mission Impossible: Dead Reckoning’ (Parts 1 and 2), and ‘World War Z,’ among others. Paramount stock was down 0.4% during Monday’s pre-market trading session. Stocktwits data showed the retail sentiment around the PARA stock was in the ‘neutral’ territory. Ellison expressed optimism in a statement, stating that the new leadership team’s experience equips the company to deliver the goods. “Together, we will foster an environment where creative and technical talent collaborate seamlessly, marrying leading technologies with powerful storytelling and artistic vision to unlock Paramount's full potential and help shape the future of our industry,” Ellison said. The company added that Ellison has overseen films with cumulative gross collections of nearly $10 billion globally. Last week, Paramount reported its second-quarter results, with earnings per share (EPS) of $0.46 on revenue of $6.85 billion. Wall Street expected an EPS of $0.36 on revenue of $6.87 billion, according to Stocktwits data. Paramount’s merger with Skydance got approval from the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) in July. PARA stock is up more than 21% year-to-date. *For updates and corrections, email newsroom[at]stocktwits[dot]com.*.
